summary: The report details an ultrasound examination of the whole abdomen and pelvis. Findings include normal liver and gallbladder, but bilateral renal parenchymal disease is noted with increased echogenicity and poor differentiation at the corticomedullary junction. No hydronephrotic changes are observed in the pelvis, and the urinary bladder and prostate are also normal. The report suggests further imaging (CT/CECT for urinary tract, MRI for pelvis) based on limitations of the ultrasound
title: Ultrasound Abdomen
